photo_observations.storefront_description: Wanda's Pie in the Sky bakery — closed, with two graffiti-covered security roll-down shutters (dark/green), green awning/canopy above, white lattice railing/fence on left side
photo_observations.signage_observed: Cyan/turquoise neon sign 'Wanda's Pie in the Sky' in cursive script above shutters; 'WANDA' tag on right shutter; heavy black graffiti bubble letters on both shutters
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Security shutters with extensive graffiti, cyan neon sign addition, green awning
photo_observations.notes: Nighttime photo (1:45am), bakery closed with shutters down. Batch_008 re-observation confirms previous analysis with additional detail on shutter graffiti and neon signage colour (cyan/turquoise, not teal). | [batch_010] Five daytime photos provide the clearest view yet of this corner building. Front and Oxford side both visible. Supplements batch_007 and batch_008 nighttime observations.

facade_description: 287 Augusta Ave presents a bay-and-gable domestic facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade is organized as a storefront base with 2 upper-window bays, using storefront (behind shutters), and 1 principal entrance. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the ear
